sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: NavigationHeader is required on every page. For a Validator at the decision stage, the minimal variant is optimal — it reduces navigation distractions and keeps a single persistent CTA (e.g., 'Upgrade Now') visible throughout the session, which is the highest-converting element on most SaaS pages.

sections.name: Hero — Conversion-Focused Value Proposition
sections.rationale: HeroPrimary opens the page by immediately establishing the core reason to upgrade. The Validator variant includes a trust_signal (e.g., 'Trusted by 10,000+ teams') above the fold, which is essential for a Validator at the decision stage who needs credibility confirmation before engaging further. The with-social-proof variant can embed a LogoStrip directly below the CTA to reinforce the decision with peer validation.

sections.name: Social Proof — Credibility Anchor
sections.rationale: Immediately after the hero, the SocialProofBar reinforces the trust claim with recognizable logos and a featured quote. The Validator variant leads with a compelling customer quote followed by logos — this sequencing matters at the decision stage because trial users are actively looking for evidence that peers in similar roles have validated this product. Positioned here, it converts the hero's promise into social evidence.

sections.name: Outcome Metrics — Quantified Proof
sections.rationale: StatBlock delivers high-impact, sourced metrics (e.g., '47% faster onboarding — Forrester 2024') that a Validator at the decision stage demands. Unsourced stats reduce trust for this archetype, so the Validator variant with source attribution is critical. The 3-stat layout provides clean visual impact. Placed after social proof, it shifts the narrative from 'others trust us' to 'here's measurable evidence why.'

sections.name: Feature Value Grid — Benefit-Oriented Capabilities
sections.rationale: FeatureGrid articulates what the paid plan unlocks using outcome-oriented benefit statements rather than raw feature names — exactly what a Validator at the decision stage needs to rationalize the upgrade. Trial users already know the product surface; this section reframes capabilities as business outcomes they'll lose access to, creating structured justification for the purchase decision.

sections.name: Customer Testimonial — Decision-Point Trust
sections.rationale: TestimonialSingle is the primary trust signal for the Validator archetype. At the decision stage, a single featured testimonial with full attribution (name, role, company, photo) placed near the pricing section acts as the final persuasion layer. The with-photo and with-company-logo variants maximize perceived authenticity. This component is explicitly designed to convert when positioned at a decision moment.

sections.name: Pricing — Plan Selection and Upgrade
sections.rationale: PricingCard is the core conversion mechanism for trial-to-paid at the decision stage. The Validator variant includes plan descriptions and longer feature lists, giving the Validator archetype the detail they need to feel confident in their selection. The highlighted variant marks the recommended plan, and the with-comparison variant supports enterprise tiers. This is where the decision converts into action.

sections.name: Final CTA — Conversion Capture with Trust Reinforcement
sections.rationale: CTABanner closes the page argument with a full-width conversion prompt. The Validator variant includes a subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'Cancel anytime · No long-term contracts') to eliminate final objections at the decision stage. The split variant works well here — directional copy on the left reaffirms the value proposition while the CTA on the right captures the action. Paired with FooterMinimal's with-cta variant below, it creates a double-recovery zone for departing Validators.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages.
Validator variant always includes trust_signal. with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster.

slots.headline: Your Trial Results, Made Permanent.
slots.subhead: Teams that upgrade from trial to paid reduce design-to-dev handoff time by 41% in the first 30 days. Here's the evidence.
slots.trust_signal: Trusted by 12,400+ product teams — 2024 customer survey

page.reasoning: Validator variant leads with quote, logos below. This establishes credibility through recognizable brand logos paired with a featured quote.

slots.headline: Peers who made the switch don't go back.
slots.quote: We evaluated three design systems over six weeks. MODE was the only one where the data on productivity gains matched what we actually saw after rollout. The trial period gave us the proof we needed.
slots.attribution: Priya Nair, Head of Product Design, Lattice

page.reasoning: Always pair stats with source attribution in Validator contexts — unsourced stats reduce trust. 3-stat is the preferred default.

stats.label: Reduction in design-to-dev handoff time within 30 days of upgrading
Faster component adoption across engineering teams vs. trial-only usage
Average onboarding time to first production-ready component on paid plan

page.reasoning: Validator benefits from specific, outcome-oriented descriptions — not feature names, benefit statements. 3-col-icon is the default; use for 6 or 9 features.

slots.headline: What the paid plan actually unlocks.
slots.subhead: These aren't trial limitations — they're the capabilities your team will build on every sprint.

features.title: Intent-Aware Tokens
features.description: Ship context-sensitive design decisions automatically — no more manual token overrides eating into sprint cycles.

features.title: Unlimited Component Workspaces
features.description: Organize every product surface without hitting workspace caps — teams with 3+ products see 38% fewer duplication errors. (Internal data, 2024)

features.title: Usage Analytics Dashboard
features.description: See exactly which components your engineers adopt, where they diverge, and why — evidence your design system is actually working.

features.title: Role-Based Access Controls
features.description: Ensure only approved design decisions reach production, reducing rogue component incidents by 52%. (2024 customer survey)

features.title: Priority Sync with Figma & Storybook
features.description: Real-time two-way sync eliminates the version mismatch that accounts for 60% of design-dev rework cycles. (G2 data, 2024)

features.title: Dedicated Onboarding Support
features.description: A named customer success contact gets your team fully embedded in under two weeks — not two quarters.

page.reasoning: Always include role and company for Validator credibility. with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity. Place near a CTA — testimonials convert when positioned at a decision moment.

slots.quote: I ran a three-month internal audit before recommending we upgrade. Component consistency improved by 34%, and our design system NPS went from 31 to 67. The numbers made the decision easy to defend to leadership.

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes description and longer feature list. highlighted variant for recommended plan drives upgrade clarity for decision-stage users.

slots.description: Everything your team needs to move from proof-of-concept to production-grade design system — with the analytics to show it's working.

page.section: Final CTA — Conversion Capture with Trust Reinforcement
page.reasoning: Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). centered placement provides terminal conversion action with trust reinforcement.

slots.headline: Your trial data doesn't lie. Neither do we.
slots.subhead: Teams that upgrade within 14 days of trial end retain 91% of their workflow improvements. Don't rebuild from scratch.
slots.trust_signal: Cancel anytime · No long-term contracts · SOC 2 Type II certified

page.section: Final CTA — Conversion Capture with Trust Reinforcement
page.reasoning: with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.
